ddls:
CREATE TABLE "samples" (
  "sample_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sample */,
  "patient_id" INTEGER /* ID of the patient the sample belongs to */,
  "collection_date" TEXT /* Date the sample was collected */,
  "collection_method" TEXT /* Method used to collect the sample (e.g., blood draw, urine collection) */,
  "sc" INTEGER /* SC antigen level in the sample */,
  "vf" INTEGER /* VF antigen level in the sample */,
  "plasma" INTEGER /* Plasma antigen level in the sample */,
  "urine_present" INTEGER /* Presence of urine in the sample (0 = absent, 1 = present) */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* ID of the location where the sample was collected */,
  "lab_id" INTEGER /* ID of the laboratory where the sample was processed */,
  "researcher_id" INTEGER /* ID of the researcher who processed the sample */,
  "storage_conditions" TEXT /* Conditions under which the sample was stored */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or observations about the sample */,
  "quality_control_passed" INTEGER /* Whether the sample passed quality control (0 = failed, 1 = passed) */,
  "analysis_date" TEXT /* Date when the sample was analyzed */,
  "analyzer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the analyzer used to process the sample */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sample_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_samples_researcher_id FOREIGN KEY ("researcher_id") REFERENCES researchers ("researcher_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_samples_analyzer_id FOREIGN KEY ("analyzer_id") REFERENCES analyzers ("analyzer_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_samples_patient_id FOREIGN KEY ("patient_id") REFERENCES patients ("patient_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_samples_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("location_id") REFERENCES locations ("location_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_samples_lab_id FOREIGN KEY ("lab_id") REFERENCES labs ("lab_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "patients" (
  "patient_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each patient */,
  "patient_name" TEXT /* Full name of the patient */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the patient */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the patient */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Date of birth of the patient */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the patient */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the patient */,
  "medical_history" TEXT /* Medical history of the patient */,
  "allergies" TEXT /* Known allergies of the patient */,
  "emergency_contact_name" TEXT /* Name of the emergency contact */,
  "emergency_contact_phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the emergency contact */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("patient_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "locations" (
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each location */,
  "location_name" TEXT /* Name of the location */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the location */,
  "contact_person" TEXT /* Name of the contact person at the location */,
  "contact_phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the contact person */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "labs" (
  "lab_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each lab */,
  "lab_name" TEXT /* Name of the lab */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the lab */,
  "contact_person" TEXT /* Name of the contact person at the lab */,
  "contact_phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the contact person */,
  "equipment" TEXT /* List of equipment available in the lab */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("lab_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "researchers" (
  "researcher_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each researcher */,
  "researcher_name" TEXT /* Full name of the researcher */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the researcher */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the researcher */,
  "specialization" TEXT /* Researcher's area of specialization */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("researcher_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "analyzers" (
  "analyzer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each analyzer */,
  "analyzer_name" TEXT /* Name of the analyzer */,
  "model" TEXT /* Model of the analyzer */,
  "manufacturer" TEXT /* Manufacturer of the analyzer */,
  "last_calibration_date" TEXT /* Date of the last calibration */,
  "next_calibration_date" TEXT /* Date of the next calibration */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("analyzer_id")
)
